question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where the query object has a parameter whose value is a non-array string of maximum length, causing 'name + '=' + normalizedValue' to produce a very large string that is appended to normalizedQuery via appendToQuery(), exhausting memory and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: appendToQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger appendToQuery() to be called with a queryValue string that is the result of joining thousands of array elements, where the resulting queryString grows to hundreds of megabytes, causing the Node.js process to run out of heap memory and crash, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getNormalizedArrayValue()] Can an unprivileged external user send a parameter array with thousands of elements where each element is a long hex string (e.g., simulating block.hash values), causing valueArray.sort() to perform expensive lexicographic comparisons and valueArray.join() to allocate a massive string, exhausting CPU and memory and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/requestNormalizer.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: normalizeRequestQueryParams()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where the query object has a parameter value that is an array with a single element that is an extremely long string (e.g., 1MB), causing getNormalizedArrayValue() to return it and appendToQuery() to concatenate it, exhausting memory and causing a non-network DoS?
